description:
You are helping a script-developer who maintains a collection of tiny shell utilities under /home/user/scripts. A new requirement has arrived: the developer wants a **regex-based log filtering utility** that extracts “critical” lines from an application log and produces both a) a filtered log, and b) a tiny CSV summary of error categories seen. Your goal is to create everything needed so that, after you are done, the following will be true: 1. The directory /home/user/scripts contains an **executable Bash script** called filter_errors.sh. 2. Running the command /home/user/scripts/filter_errors.sh /home/user/logs/app_2023-08-15.log /home/user/output produces exactly two files inside /home/user/output: • critical_errors.log (the raw filtered lines) • error_summary.csv (the category counts) 3. The filtered log must contain **only** the lines that match at least one of these regexes (case-sensitive): • ERROR • FATAL • HTTP\/1\.1 5[0-9]{2} 4. Preserve the **original line order** in critical_errors.log. Do not alter the text, spacing, or timestamps. 5. The CSV summary must have the exact, comma-separated format shown below, including the header row and newline endings (\n). The script must classify each filtered line into exactly one of the following categories (first matching rule wins): • lines that contain the substring “Payment failed” → category PAYMENT_FAILURE • lines that match HTTP\/1\.1 5[0-9]{2} → category HTTP_5XX • lines that contain the substring “FATAL” → category FATAL If a line matches more than one rule, use the first rule in the list above. Expected CSV layout example (counts will be verified by the grader): 
error_category,count
PAYMENT_FAILURE,2
HTTP_5XX,1
FATAL,1
6. The script must: • Accept exactly two positional arguments: <input_log> and <output_dir>. • Create <output_dir> if it does not already exist. • Overwrite critical_errors.log and error_summary.csv if they already exist. • Use only standard POSIX utilities available in a minimal Linux environment (grep, sed, awk, cut, sort, etc.). 7. When finished, append a short execution log to /home/user/filter_run.log containing one line with this exact pipe-separated format: 
YYYY-MM-DD|HH:MM:SS|input=<absolute_input_path>|output_dir=<absolute_output_dir>|status=OK
• Use the current local time for the timestamp. • Do NOT write anything else to that file. Each subsequent run of the script should add a new line. 8. Keep the number of terminal commands around 5–10: making directories, writing the script, giving it execute permission, running it, and (optionally) inspecting the outputs. Notes & hints (do NOT reveal these to the grader; they are just guidance): • Use mkdir -p for the output directory. • grep -E or awk can perform the initial filter. • For the CSV, consider piping through awk and aggregating with sort | uniq -c or an awk associative array. • Remember to chmod +x the script. • You can use date '+%F|%T' to generate the timestamp for the execution log. When the grader checks your work it will: a) run the script as shown above, b) verify that critical_errors.log, error_summary.csv, and filter_run.log exist, c) check that the contents exactly match the expected data and formats. Perform all steps in the terminal, adhering strictly to the file names, paths, and formats described here.
truth:
Initial state (already present before the agent starts):

1. Directory: /home/user/logs
2. File: /home/user/logs/app_2023-08-15.log  
Exact contents (9 lines, each ending with a single '\n'):

[2023-08-15 09:15:23] INFO User 123 logged in
[2023-08-15 09:15:24] DEBUG Cache miss for key 'profile_123'
[2023-08-15 09:15:25] ERROR Payment failed for order 987: insufficient_funds
[2023-08-15 09:15:26] WARN Slow query detected (850ms) on endpoint /api/items
[2023-08-15 09:15:27] INFO HTTP/1.1 200 OK
[2023-08-15 09:15:28] ERROR HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
[2023-08-15 09:15:29] FATAL OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
[2023-08-15 09:15:30] INFO User 456 logged out
[2023-08-15 09:15:31] ERROR Payment failed for order 988: card_expired

Required final state after the agent completes the task:

1. /home/user/scripts/filter_errors.sh exists, is executable (mode 755 or 750 acceptable), and implements behaviour exactly as in the public description.

2. Running:
/home/user/scripts/filter_errors.sh /home/user/logs/app_2023-08-15.log /home/user/output
must create/overwrite:

2a. /home/user/output/critical_errors.log  
Contents (4 lines, each ending with '\n'):

[2023-08-15 09:15:25] ERROR Payment failed for order 987: insufficient_funds
[2023-08-15 09:15:28] ERROR HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error
[2023-08-15 09:15:29] FATAL OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
[2023-08-15 09:15:31] ERROR Payment failed for order 988: card_expired

2b. /home/user/output/error_summary.csv  
Contents (4 lines, each ending with '\n'):

error_category,count
PAYMENT_FAILURE,2
HTTP_5XX,1
FATAL,1

3. /home/user/filter_run.log exists and its last (newest) line matches the regex:  
^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}\|[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\|input=/home/user/logs/app_2023-08-15\.log\|output_dir=/home/user/output\|status=OK$

(No trailing whitespace.)

The grader will:
• compare /home/user/output/critical_errors.log byte-for-byte to the expected content above  
• compare /home/user/output/error_summary.csv byte-for-byte to the expected content above  
• verify that /home/user/filter_run.log contains at least one line and that its final line matches the regex above.

Directory /home/user/output does NOT exist beforehand, so the agent/script must create it.

The agent has write permissions in /home/user, /home/user/scripts, /home/user/logs, and any new sub-directories it creates. No root privileges are available.
